title: Podman build on OSX can write secrets on a COPY
body:
### Issue Description

Running `COPY . .` will add the podman-build-secret* files to the layer at their full path

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Project folder
  ```
tree -a
.
├── .dockerignore
├── Dockerfile
├── file_to_add
└── file_to_ignore
  ```
2. Dockerfile
```
FROM busybox

COPY . .
```
3. Build command
```
podman build -f Dockerfile -t test_image --secret=id=mysecret,env=MYSECRET
```



### Describe the results you received

```
Permission     UID:GID       Size  Filetree
-rw-r--r--         0:0       15 B  ├── .dockerignore
-rw-r--r--         0:0       23 B  ├── Dockerfile
drwxr-xr-x         0:0        0 B  ├── Users
drwxr-xr-x         0:0        0 B  │   └── chris
drwxr-xr-x         0:0        0 B  │       └── Downloads
drwxr-xr-x         0:0        0 B  │           └── test_podman
-rw-------         0:0        0 B  │               └── podman-build-secret-460352077
```

### Describe the results you expected

These would not be present in the image

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Running on OSX on an M1 macbook

### Additional information

I tried ignoring the files by adding `podman-build-secret*` like my gitignore but there seems to be a defect for that https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25314

I can work around by adding `Users` to the dockerignore file but that's brittle if anyone on my team is using a different OS or a different location for projects.

comments.body: Just noticed that the secret is 0 bytes (the env var wasn't set) Re-tried with the variable set and the contents do make it to the image
